title: LGBTQ2+ Community Centers - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > LGBTQ2+ Community Centers 6 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ALWAYS HERE BOOKSTORE MUTUAL AID LOCKERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mutual aid lockers that provide supplies such as gender-affirming gear (chest binders, bras), informational pamphlets on how to safely use gender-affirming clothes, socks and underwear, makeup, menstrual products, Narcan, contraception, (including emergency contraception), and hand sanitizer. Also occasionally offers trans tape, breast forms, tucking underwear, and other trans shapeware. Supplies availability is dependent on donations. 4555 N Williams Avenue Portland, OR 97217 Eligibility: Individuals in the LGBTQIA+ community Hours: Wednesday-Sunday 11am-6pm Email (503) 208-4799 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOWER COLUMBIA Q CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resources and support services to the LGBTQ2SIA+ community and allies of the Lower Columbia region. Services include a community center, education opportunities, mentorship, and monthly support groups for adults, youth, and families. Limited financial assistance is available as funds allow for gender-affirming costs, name changes, hormone treatments, and other expenses approved on a case-by-case basis. Financial assistance is limited to once per year, with a $200 limit. 171 W Bond Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Community Center walk in hours: Tuesday 2pm-5pm, Thursday noon-4pm. Other hours vary by service. Email (503) 468-5155 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARIE EQUI CENTER LGBTQIA2S+ PEER SUPPORT DROP-IN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF LGBTQIA2S+ peer drop-in center provides resources, survival and harm reduction supplies, community connection, community health, and basic wound care. Offers peer support and case management to support community members in navigating systems of care, insurance systems, and direct connections to recovery and housing organizations. 4434 SE 25th Avenue Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Unhoused and low-income LGBTQIA2S+ community members Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-4pm. Closed Monday, May 4, 2026-Friday, May 8, 2026 for staff development. Email (503) 459-2584 Get Directions Visit Website More Details Q CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community event space and offers groups oriented around topics such as sexual orientation, gender identity, addiction and mental health supports, and other topics. Groups are offered both in-person or online. Also provides specialized information and referral. 4115 N Mississippi Avenue Portland, OR 97217 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: By appointment only Email (503) 234-7837 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE LAVENDER NETWORK LGBTQ2+ SERVICE AND COMMUNITY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community center and event space for LGTBQ2+ individuals and local organizations focused on LGBTQ2+ services. Services include information about community resources and a clothing closet with gender-affirming items. 1590 Willamette Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm (541) 342-7105 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRUE COLORS RECOVERY RECOVERY MENTORING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides substance use recovery peer services led by professionals with lived experience, who help participants create a personalized recovery pathway. The program also offers a community center where individuals can access mutual aid groups, on-site STI and Hepatitis C testing, skill-building opportunities, and resource fairs. 3807 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who identify LGBTQ2+ and have a substance use disorder Hours: 7 days per week 2pm-10pm Email (971) 252-1943 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ALWAYS HERE BOOKSTORE MUTUAL AID LOCKERS LOWER COLUMBIA Q CENTER LOWER COLUMBIA Q CENTER MARIE EQUI CENTER LGBTQIA2S+ PEER SUPPORT DROP-IN Q CENTER Q CENTER THE LAVENDER NETWORK LGBTQ2+ SERVICE AND COMMUNITY CENTER TRUE COLORS RECOVERY RECOVERY MENTORING
